results.title: Prem Suhas
highlights:
Data Science Engineer at [Southern Company](https://www.linkedin.com/company/southern-company)
...
I’m a data scientist who thrives on transforming complexity into clarity. With over 5 years of experience across finance, healthcare, and retail, I design intelligent systems that not only predict outcomes, but power smarter decisions. My work bridges ML, statistical rigor, and stakeholder alignment to bring out the story behind the data.📊 Whether I’m training neural networks to streamline customer service, leveraging NLP to enhance chatbot accuracy, or visualizing trends in Power BI, my focus remains the same: actionable insight with measurable impact. At Burke America, I developed deep learning models that boosted service prediction accuracy and automated resolution paths. At Truist Financial, I built recommender systems that increased product adoption. At CitiBank, I saved thousands of work hours by forecasting operational bottlenecks using logistic regression. My toolkit includes Python, SQL, TensorFlow, Azure, and tools like Tableau and Snowflake. But more than tools, I bring curiosity, accountability, and a habit of turning “what if” into “what works.”🌐 Let’s connect if you’re interested in building scalable, human-centered AI solutions—or simply want to geek out over anomaly detection and dashboards.💡

results.title: Yadong Chiang
highlights:
Staff Data Scientist at [Southern Company](https://www.linkedin.com/company/southern-company)
...
I specialize in building production-ready AI/ML systems that bridge the gap between complex physical processes and digital intelligence. With a background in Engineering, Computer Science, and years of experience at Intel, I design end-to-end MLOps pipelines that don't just work in a lab—they scale in the real world. What I bring to the table: 1. Production AI at Scale: I’ve deployed image-based models and cloud MLOps systems that reduced manual review by up to 96%, driving significant operational savings. 2. Physical-Digital Synergy: My PhD work in Digital Twins allows me to blend physics-based simulation with deep learning to optimize smart manufacturing and infrastructure. 3. End-to-End Leadership: From initial DOE and predictive modeling to CI/CD, model monitoring, and mentoring junior teams, I ensure AI delivers measurable, sustainable impact. I’m driven by the challenge of turning experimentation into reliable, production-grade solutions that make industry smarter and safer. Core Expertise: Data Science | Cloud MLOps (Azure, Databricks) | Deep Learning (PyTorch) | Predictive Maintenance | Digital Twin Modeling | Generative AI | Process Optimization

results.title: Jordan Jones
highlights:
Data Scientist II at Southern Company
...
Data Scientist II at [Southern Company](https://www.linkedin.com/company/southern-company)
...
As a Data Scientist at Southern Company, I apply advanced analytics, statistical modeling, and machine learning techniques to drive insights that support innovation and operational efficiency in the energy sector. My work focuses on solving complex business problems with data-driven solutions—ranging from demand forecasting and anomaly detection to customer behavior analysis and optimization strategies. Previously, I worked as a Data Quality Analyst at LOG-NET, Inc., where I ensured the integrity of supply chain data through robust validation, auditing, and monitoring processes. I leveraged data profiling and analytical techniques to uncover trends and recommend process improvements that supported data-driven decision-making. I hold a Bachelor of Applied Science in Computer Science from Birmingham-Southern College, where I built a foundation in algorithms, data structures, and machine learning. I am currently pursuing a Master’s in Information Technology at Kennesaw State University to further develop my technical skill set and leadership potential in data science and analytics. My experience also includes a Data Science Internship at DealNews, where I developed machine learning models and created visualizations in Tableau to support strategic decisions across departments. Driven by curiosity and a passion for problem-solving, I thrive at the intersection of data, technology, and impact. I’m committed to continuous learning and creating value through smart, scalable data solutions.

### CyberShield AI – Intelligent Threat Detection Platform
...
CyberShield AI is a proposed intelligent security platform designed to enhance threat detection and risk monitoring within enterprise and supply-chain environments. The project focuses on using data analytics and machine learning concepts to identify anomalous behavior, improve visibility into system vulnerabilities, and support proactive security decision-making. The solution was designed around structured data pipelines, risk indicators, and model-driven insights to help organizations move beyond reactive, rule-based security approaches. Emphasis was placed on system architecture, data integrity, scalability, and explainability, ensuring the platform could support real-world operational and compliance requirements. The project also explored how analytics dashboards and automated alerts could translate complex security data into clear, actionable intelligence for technical and non-technical stakeholders. Technologies & Tools: Python, Data Analytics, Machine Learning (Conceptual), SQL, System Design, Risk Analysis, Power BI / Data Visualization Show less

Strategic Data & Analytics Leader with 25+ years of experience architecting enterprise data platforms across the oil, gas, and power industries. Currently serving as Infrastructure Data Steward within the Technology Organization – Infrastructure Services (IS), I design, implement, and govern the data architecture that underpins critical infrastructure technologies including data centers, networks, servers, and storage. I specialize in ensuring infrastructure data — asset, operational, and related datasets — is accurate, consistent, secure, and accessible. By championing data quality and stewardship, I enable reliable reporting, analytics, and operational excellence across the enterprise. My expertise spans SQL, Python, Power BI, PostgreSQL, and cloud platforms (Azure, Databricks), with hands‑on experience in data modeling, integration, and governance frameworks. to streamline operations, improve resource allocation, and reduce bottlenecks in high-volume distribution centers. involving shipment forecasting and warehouse automation for building materials. I developed forecasting

results.title: [Remote] Senior Generative AI Scientist II | Wfh
highlights:
# [Remote] Senior Generative AI Scientist II
...
Note: The job is a remote job and is open to candidates in USA. Cotiviti is a healthcare technology company focused on delivering better care at lower costs through advanced technology and data analytics. The Senior Generative AI Scientist II will work with a team to solve healthcare issues using machine learning and AI techniques, delivering value through innovative data solutions. Responsibilities • Work with key stakeholders within Research and Development as well as Business Operations, along with Product Management to assess the potential value and risks associated with business problems that have the potential to be solved using machine learning and Artificial Intelligence techniques • Develop an exploratory data analysis approach to verify the initial hypothesis associated with potential Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning use cases • Document your approach, thinking and results in standard approaches to allow other data scientists to collaborate with you on this work • Prepare your final trained model and develop a validation test set for QA • Work with ML Ops/Production operations to deploy your model into production and support them in monitoring model performance • Participate in design sessions to continuously develop and improve the Cotiviti machine learning platform • Participate in other data science initiatives, collaborating with your peers to support their projects • Participate in knowledge sharing sessions to bring new insights and technologies to the team • Provide End to End value-based solutions, including data pipeline, model creation and application for end user consumption • Complete all responsibilities as outlined in the annual performance review and/or goal setting • Complete all special projects and other duties as assigned • Must be able to perform duties with or without reasonable accommodation Skills • Graduate degree in a quantitative discipline such as Computer Science/Engineering, Statistics, Operations Research covering Advanced Statistics, Machine learning and AI • Experience with the latest techniques in natural language processing including transformers, fine-tuning LLMs, measuring/benchmarking and deploying LLMs with tools such as HuggingFace, Langchain, LLAMA/Mistral and OpenAI, vector databases • 5+ years of hands-on data science/AI experience, using typical machine learning and data science tools including pandas, scikit-learn, keras, nltk, and TensorFlow/PyTorch, GPU • Experience building production-grade machine learning deployments on AWS, Azure, or GCP • Experience working with Apache Spark™ and large-scale distributed datasets • Experience communicating technical concepts to non-technical and technical audiences is a plus • Flexibility to work with global teams as well geographically dispersed US based teams • Professional with ability to properly handle confidential information • Ability to work within a matrixed organization • Proficiency in all required skills and competencies above Benefits • Medical • Dental • Vision • Disability • Life insurance coverage • 401(k) savings plans • Paid family leave • 9 paid holidays per year • 17-27 days of Paid Time Off (PTO) per year, depending on specific level and length of service with Cotiviti. Company Overview • Cotiviti enables healthcare organizations to deliver better care at lower cost through advanced technology and data analytics that improve the quality and sustainability of healthcare in the United States. It was founded in 1979, and is headquartered in South Jordan, UT, US, with a workforce of 5001-10000 employees. Its website is Company H1B Sponsorship • Cotiviti has a track record of offering H1B sponsorships, with 165 in 2025, 118 in 2024, 90 in 2023, 102 in 2022, 72 in 2021, 67 in 2020. Please note that this does not guarantee sponsorship for this specific role.

results.title: Machine Learning Scientist, AI Explainability | Hirenixa
highlights: Learning Scientist, AI Explainability About Us: SES AI Corp. (NYSE: SES) is dedicated to accelerating the world's energy transition through groundbreaking material discovery and advanced battery management. We are at the forefront of revolutionizing battery creation, pioneering the integration of cutting-edge machine learning into our research and development. Our AI-enhanced, high-energy-density and high-power-density Li-Metal and Li-ion batteries are unique; they are the first in the world to utilize electrolyte materials discovered by AI. This powerful combination of "AI for science" and material engineering enables batteries that can be used across various applications, including transportation (land and air), energy storage, robotics, and drones. To learn more about us, please visit: www.ses.ai What We Offer: • A highly competitive salary and robust benefits package, including comprehensive health coverage and an attractive equity/stock options program within our NYSE-listed company. • The opportunity to contribute directly to a meaningful scientific project—accelerating the global energy transition—with a clear and broad public impact. • Work in a dynamic, collaborative, and innovative environment at the intersection of AI and material science, driving the next generation of battery technology. • Significant opportunities for professional growth and career development as you work alongside leading experts in AI, R&D, and engineering. • Access to state-of-the-art facilities and proprietary technologies are used to discover and deploy AI-enhanced battery solutions. What we Need: The SES AI Prometheus team (AI Research) is seeking an exceptional Machine Learning Scientist to spearhead the development of our Large Language Models (LLM) and advanced AI agents. This role is pivotal in enabling groundbreaking research in machine learning for scientific discovery, particularly in the realm of material science and battery technology. • Harness internal expertise and collaborate with external research labs to advance scientific ML. • Work will be incorporated directly into our groundbreaking Deep Space multi-agent system for battery technology discovery. • This position can be remote. Essential Duties and Responsibilities: • Research & Development • Lead cutting-edge research in machine learning for scientific discovery, with a focus on (multimodal) Large Language Models and their application (including AI agents) in battery and material discovery. • Conduct groundbreaking research on integrating domain-specific data (including literature and internal documents) into LLM training and inference. Investigate the mechanisms through which LLMs approach problem-solving, planning, and solution generation, particularly in the context of basic battery design questions. • Model Optimization & Implementation • Troubleshoot and optimize the training process of large language models, addressing complexities and challenges related to data quality, model architecture, and computational efficiency. • Implement innovative solutions to enhance model performance and scalability. Collaborate closely with a multidisciplinary team to integrate findings into practical AI solutions that contribute to the discovery of new battery materials and the advancement of lithium battery technology. • Collaboration & Communication • Contribute to academic and industry discussions by publishing research findings in top-tier journals and presenting at conferences. • Engage in machine learning research aimed at addressing battery design challenges and enhancing system ability to interpret data-driven science efficiently. • The ability to communicate complex concepts clearly and effectively to both technical and non-technical team members. Education and/or Experience: • MS or PhD in Computer Science, Statistics, Computational Neuroscience, Cognitive Science or a related field, or equivalent practical experience. • Strong

results.title: Software Engineer Scientist – Career Pathways Center | Texas Southern University
highlights:
Description: CSLabs is seeking a software engineer/Scientist with a foundation in physics, mathematics, or engineering. Unlike typical software development roles, this position emphasizes the engineering discipline of software creation — applying structured, principled approaches to high-performance computing (HPC), artificial intelligence (AI), machine learning (ML), and advanced computer vision projects. The successful candidate will support a team of PhD scientists and lead software engineers.You will work on cutting-edge technologies, including mixed reality (MR) 3D point cloud visualization, AI-enhanced model optimization, AI algorithms for synthetic data generation and object detection, and autonomous maritime software systems. Work may include development of swarm autonomy algorithms, sensor fusion pipelines, distributed coordination systems, and AI-assisted path-finding techniques (A*), navigation and guidance software, real-time analytical systems, and multi-agent autonomy frameworks supporting the Department of War (DoW) and commercial applications.
...
Responsibilities:•Develop and optimize software for HPC, AI/ML, image processing, computer vision, autonomy, and real-time analytical systems•Support MR-based 3D point cloud ingestion, visualization, multi-user collaboration, and physical interference detection workflows•Contribute to AI training pipelines using synthetic data generation, deep learning models, and model evaluation workflows•Develop autonomy software for UMV systems, including path planning, navigation, swarm coordination, and multi-agent decision support•Implement algorithms such as A*, AI-assisted pathfinding approaches, sensor fusion, object detection, feature extraction, and 3D scene reconstruction•Design scalable, high-performance software systems that integrate with real-time rendering engines, AI frameworks, simulation environments, and distributed software architectures
...
Desired Skills:•Experience with multithreaded, multicore, and/or GPU programming•Proficiency with Linux and Windows environments•Strong experience with object-oriented design, design patterns, and version control systems (e.g., Git)•Familiarity with scientific Python libraries (NumPy, Pandas, Matplotlib, Scikit-learn, SciPy)•Hands-on experience with OpenCV, Scikit-Image, or related computer vision libraries•Knowledge of AI/ML frameworks (PyTorch, TensorFlow, Keras) and deep learning model training workflows•Experience with AR/VR/MR frameworks (OpenXR, Unity, Unreal Engine)•Experience working with LiDAR and 3D point cloud data•Familiarity with 3D object detection and synthetic dataset generation•Experience with CAD, 3D modeling, engineering design tools, mesh generation, or integrating CAD/3D assets into simulation, visualization, or real-time rendering environments•Experience using CMake for cross-platform software builds•Interest or past experience in modeling and simulation, maritime autonomy, or game engine development
